texto: Januz feels glad to have her in his arms--his wife, who would do anything to protect their son. This is how she presents herself. Like a soldier who would kill for her country. And her country is their son.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Amanda Hodgkinson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a amor. O trecho “Januz feels glad to have her in his arms--his wife, who would do anything to protect their son. T…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, love, loyalty pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de loyalty e war.

texto: He'd imagined peacetime would bring him a sense of belonging. During the war it kept him going, that thought of peace. He'd believed in it like a season he knew it would arrive one day

texto: Memories shrink. Like a soap bar used over and over, they become deformed, weaker scented, too slight and slippery to hold.
